capabilities: Implement input sanitization by leveraging agent reasoning derived knowledge bases to dynamically adapt sanitization rules based on contextual understanding.

procedure.action: Compose agent reasoning derived knowledge base by invoking the sanitize_input function with context-aware parameters; ensure sanitized_input is returned with no errors by composing module interfaces via adapter pattern; assert input/output type contracts match before connecting; assert integration_error_count < 1 after contract validation.
Execute domain-specific sanitization rules by executing sanitize_input with domain constraints; verify sanitized_input conforms to schema using JSON Schema draft-07.

Log sanitization process by recording operation_duration_ms and result_code in the audit trail; ensure logs are stored in a secure, append-only format using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.
Assert sanitized input by running security checks using OWASP ZAP; assert that no vulnerabilities are detected in the sanitized_input.
Observe sanitization effectiveness by setting up alerts for error_count > 0; trigger incident response if threshold is exceeded.

summary: Specialize input sanitization by integrating agent reasoning derived knowledge bases to enhance security measures, ensuring sanitized inputs meet domain-specific constraints.
title: Input Sanitization

failureConditions: Security vulnerabilities arise when input sanitization fails to adapt to dynamic contexts, causing injection attacks.
Data integrity issues occur when sanitized inputs do not conform to domain-specific constraints, leading to processing errors.
Audit trail gaps emerge when logging is incomplete, causing difficulties in tracing sanitization processes during security audits.

that no vulnerabilities are detected in the sanitized_input
successConditions: sanitized_input conforms to JSON Schema draft-07; assert error_rate < 0.001 and operation_completed = true.
